entry.title: Mass Upload Data for Manage Teams and Responsibilities Application
entry.content:
Your requirement is to mass maintain teams in Manage Teams and Responsibilities Fiori application.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mass Upload Data for Manage Teams and Responsibilitie...
entry.content:
For creating many teams in bulk, you can use the write API service CA_RSM_TEAM_MANAGE. 
This service enables you to create, update, delete teams and their details in Responsibility Management.  

This API is meant for initial loading and very infrequent updates or deletions to teams.
Do not use it to update or delete teams on a daily or a periodic basis. 

It is also recommended to keep the payload at an optimum level.

You can refer to the below help document on write API for more details
Responsibility Management Teams | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Learning User remains registered to a class even after substitute credit was provided for the class
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a scenario where there are  in-person and virtual classes for the same course. If the user attends the virtual course, substitute credit will be given to the in-person class and vice versa.

However, when the user completes the virtual class and if he/she has already registered for the in person ones, the class registration is not removed after the substitute credit is given.

The class is removed from the users learning plan and is shown as completed in learning history . But when the instructor/admin takes the class roster the user is shown as enrolled in the class. The class also shows in the users registration tab in Learning Admin. There is no option for the user to withdraw the registration as the class is not available in the users my learning and is moved to history. 

Ideally when substitute credit is given for a course, the registration should be removed or the user should have the option to atleast withdraw from the class from learning home.

Has any of you encountered this issue before? Is there an option already available to correct this.?

Appreciate your suggestions.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: How to Display an Image on the Selection Screen in SAP ABAP
entry.content:
@SOUHAIL_FAIRADI Thanks! Glad it worked for you .
Yes, you're right — if the picture appears on all pages instead of just the selection screen, you need to disable or hide the logo after the selection screen is processed.

FORM disable_logo.
  IF picture_control_1 IS BOUND.
    CALL METHOD picture_control_1->free.
    FREE picture_control_1.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

Call this FORM in the event block where your main processing starts (like AT SELECTION-SCREEN or right at the start of START-OF-SELECTION) to make sure the logo is cleared after the selection screen is displayed.

I hope this helps! Let me know your thoughts or if you have any questions.

Thanks,

Akshay Anil 
Technical Consultant, SAP ABAP | BTP | Fiori | Ui5

entry.title: CF Deployment failed with DCL files deployment in Cloud Identity Service instance BTP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I was having issues deploying my CAP application along with DCL files for achieving RBAC & ABAC (Autorization checks) to BTP, running into a deploy issue:

So I started from scratch with a simple application: 

- Install a fresh Trial Account

- Create a HANA DB (mapping to CF dev space)

- Create a simple CAP application in BAS, single table, simple service to display table entries

- Add hana (for production), Add mta

- Create MTA file

- Deploy to CF

I referred the below url’s for adding & deploying  DCL files with AMS authoization checks(RBAC & ABAC).

 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/cap-java-implementation-for-authorization-management-service-ams/ba-p/13972405
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/authorization-management-service-in-sap-cloud-identity-service/bc-p/13966914#M177778

 

All vanilla steps, explained in many blogs and CAPIRE. Nothing special, no manipulation of any package.json or any other files. 

However, no matter what I do. I always run into a CF deploy issue  :

I am trying to explore the concept of AMS(Authoriztion Management System) with RBAC& ABAC. I have created the CAPM application in SAP BTP by using BAS(Business Application Studio). Also, I have configured the deployment properties in mta.yaml file . The application is deployed with Cloud Identity Service instance in CF(Cloud Foundry ) environment with out any issues. But, DCL files are not deployed and getting the errors . I tried out in different ways and failed. Added the logs below for your reference:

 
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.168784] Invoking pre-start scripts. (STDOUT, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.318862] Invoking start command. (STDOUT, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.632962] npm error code ENOENT (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.633116] npm error syscall open (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#

[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.633302] npm error path /home/vcap/app/package.json (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#

[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.633751] npm error enoent Could not read package.json: Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/home/vcap/app/package.json' (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#
#2025 04 09 12:08:40.398#Z#DEBUG#c49d6141-153a-11f0-9569-eeee0a831419.btp-cap-java-cloud-identity-demo-main-ams-policies-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-134#
[PollStageAppStatusExecution] 46d8770d-e2ca-45c8-957e-2546dfdd5277 [2025-04-09T11:58:35.633810] npm error enoent This is related to npm not being able to find a file. (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-dcl)#

I can't find any valuable information on this error, other than some non SAP related blogs mentioning that the stacks are not available on CF. As I did a clean install on a Trial account I assume the BTP environment is installed correctly. 

Would be great if somebody could provide some hints as I'm not able to deploy any application with DCL files  to BTP.

Regards,

Umasankarseelan

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Tetsuya Kawahara
entry.content:
Please scroll down for the English version.

皆さん、こんにちは。アマゾン ウェブ サービス ジャパンの河原です。実務ではほとんどお付き合いがないですが、Japan SAP Users' Group (JSUG) や SNS を通じてお世話になっている SAP ジャパンの木下さん (@Shiroh44) から SAP Community の Blog It Forward (BIF) で指名されたので、久しぶりにブログを書いています。木下さんの BIF ブログはこちらを、BIF の詳細はこちらをご覧ください。

少し前に非常に興味深い記事「How Many People in Japan Speak English?」を読みました。高度な英語力を保有する日本人は 2 % しかいません。また、日本人の 31 - 41 % はまったく英語が話せません。これが、このブログをまずは日本語で書いている背景です。

私について

私の SAP ビジネスを中心としたキャリアを簡単に振り返ってみます。私も日本人らしく英語は得意ではなく、英語で仕事をしたくないから (できないから)、大学を卒業後に日本企業である富士通に就職しました。最近は変わりつつありますが、企業が卒業予定の学生を対象に年度ごとに求人する新卒一括採用、および終身雇用を前提としている雇用慣行も日本のユニークな文化ではないでしょうか。その富士通では SAP コンピテンスセンターに所属して、自社製品と様々な ISV 製品を組み合わせた SAP 向けプラットフォームソリューションの開発や市場展開を推進してきました。Microsoft, Oracle, Red Hat, VMware といった OS/DB あるいは仮想化テクノロジーを提供するパートナーだけでなく、バックアップや運用管理、セキュリティ製品などを提供する ISV とも連携し、日本でいち早く新製品や新技術を検証して、ソリューション化、お客様導入などを進めてきました。また、その当時から JSUG テクニカル部会のオピニオンリーダーとして、日本の SAP コミュニティで活動もしてきました。JSUG 国際派遣プログラムで SAP Tech Ed にもアメリカ (ラスベガス)、スペイン (バルセロナ)、インド (バンガロール) と何度も現地参加をさせてもらいました。この写真も SAP TechEd の会場で撮影してもらったものです。SAP Basis としてインフラストラクチャ全般およびパートナービジネスに幅広く取り組めたことが、今のキャリアの基礎となっています。

エンタープライズ領域でクラウドが台頭し始めた 2015 年に現職のアマゾン ウェブ サービス ジャパンに転職し、現在は SAP を含む AWS ソフトウェアパートナーとのテクノロジーアライアンスを担当するパートナーソリューションアーキテクトチームの責任者を務めています。従来型の Amazon EC2 High Memory インスタンスを用いた大規模 SAP S/4HANA on AWS の推進に加え、RISE with SAP on AWS や SAP BTP on AWS などの新しいビジネスにも取り組んでいます。AWS Launch Wizard for SAP を用いれば SAP 環境のデプロイメントもステップバイステップで自動化できますし、RISE with SAP ではそもそも SAP 社がマネージドサービスとして提供しますし、SAP Basis の在り方も変わってきました。私たちの仕事は今後どうなっていくのでしょうか。基幹系システムである SAP を中心に、周辺システムをクラウドネイティブに設計・構築・運用できるスキルが求められています。SAP を突き詰めていくキャリアもあるでしょうが、私はせっかく AWS に携わっているので、包括的にテクノロジーとビジネスを追うように心がけています。例えば、日本で SaaS をもっと広げていくために、AWS SaaS Factory を率いる Tod Golding の著書を翻訳した「マルチテナント SaaS アーキテクチャの構築 - 原則、ベストプラクティス、AWS アーキテクチャパターン」を出版したり、AWS Marketplace によるクラウドマーケットプレイスのビジネスを推進したり、活動の幅をさらに広げています。

業務外では、私は音楽が、特にハードロックとヘヴィメタル (HR/HM) が好きです。中学生の頃からエレキギターを弾いています。今の愛機は、永遠のギターヒーロー Eddie Van Halen モデルの Wolfgang Special です。これは完全に私見ですが、日本の IT エンジニアは HR/HM 好きが多い気がします。SAP と言えばドイツですが、ハロウィン、ガンマレイ、スコーピオンズ、ブラインドガーディアン、フェアウォーニング、エドガイなどなど、ジャーマンメタルも日本で人気があります。これは、日本とドイツでメロディの感覚が似ているからだと思っています。バッハやベートーヴェンといったクラシックの流れを汲んだ緻密な音作りと叙情的なメロディラインが、心の琴線に触れます。

2019 年末からのコロナ禍で世界が一変しましたが、日本政府が 2023 年 5 月に新型コロナウィルス感染症を 5 類に移行して以来、日常が戻ってきています。コロナ禍では海外アーティストの来日がすべて中止となり、会える時に会うことの重要性を実感しました。経験や体験は何事にも代えられません。最近だと MR.BIG のフェアウェルツアーを締めくくる日本武道館での The BIG Finale! Forever In Our Hearts など、行けるライブには行くようにしています。フェアウェルツアーではないにしても、今回の機会を逃したら次はいつ来日するか、次も同じメンバーで来日するかは分かりません。「推しは推せるときに推せ」です！

それでは、最後に BIF の質問をいくつかピックアップして回答していきたいと思います。

もし 20 歳に戻れるとしたら、何を学びますか？

やはり英語です。20 歳どころか、英語がネイティブな国に生まれたら、今の仕事をする上でどれだけ楽だったろうと思います。日本では日本語で大学の高等教育まで受けられるため、主体的に動かないと英語を学ぶ機会も使う機会もあまりありません。最近は機械翻訳の品質も上がってきたので多少は楽になっていますが、日本語と英語では、文章を書いたり議論したりしたときの生産性が 10 倍は違う感覚です。アメリカの Foreign Service Institute (FSI) が公開している英語話者にとって難しい言語ランキングでは、日本語はもっとも難しいカテゴリー 4 になっています。英語の語順が SVO 型に対して日本語は SOV 型が基本のため話の組み立て方が異なりますし、日本語の発音は「あ・い・う・え・お」の 5 種類しかないため英語を話すどころか聞き取りすら難しくしています。日本人が英語を学ぶための時間はどれだけあっても足りません。

仕事で最も楽しいことは何ですか？またその理由は？

常に新しい内容に取り組むことです。キャリアの箇所で触れましたが、これまで SAP Basis あるいはソリューションアーキテクトとして様々な新製品や新技術に携わってきました。自社製品だけでなくパートナーソリューションを組み合わせて、お客様のビジネス課題を解決できるオファリングを共同開発するのは、とてもやりがいのある仕事です。AWS には 200 を超えるクラウドサービスがあり、AWS Marketplace には 70 以上の製品カテゴリーにわたる 20,000 を超えるパートナーソリューションがあります。このパートナーコミュニティは無限の可能性を秘めています。アマゾンのリーダーシップ・プリンシプルの一つに「Learn and Be Curious - リーダーは学ぶことに貪欲で、常に自分自身の向上を目指し続けます。新たな可能性に好奇心を持ち、探求します」があります。いつまでも知的好奇心に貪欲でいたいものです。

あなたのトピックの未来をどのように想像または予測しますか？

クラウドファーストから AI ファーストの時代がきています。生成 AI は、サービスに付加価値をつけて顧客体験を向上したり、レポートやドキュメント作成などの定型業務を効率化したり、コーディングからテスト、修正、運用までを自動化して開発生産性を向上したり、既にビジネスに欠かせないものになっています。これが Agentic AI の世界になると、エージェントが自律的に複数の複雑なタスクを意思決定しながら遂行してくれるようになります。「SaaS is Dead」の議論が話題になっていますが、これは SaaS がなくなるわけではなく、SaaS と AI の組み合わせが当たり前になり、エージェントがビジネス課題を解決できる SaaS を呼び出す方向に進化することだと考えています。エージェントに選ばれる SaaS をどう作るか、エージェントが呼び出せるように API や Model Context Protocol (MCP) サーバーをどう提供するか、製品の在り方や顧客体験を再設計する必要がでてくるかもしれません。この観点で、SAP の製品戦略がどうなっていくか予想してみるのも面白いと思います。オンプレミスからクラウドに、パッケージから SaaS に、そして AI はプロンプトからエージェントに、この時代の転換点を楽しみたいものです。

私の BIF ブログはいかがだったでしょうか。次は、「SAP のすみっこさん」こと大塚さん (@sumikko) を指名します。すみっこさんは、日本の SAP 界隈のブロガーとして名を馳せ、そこで培った人脈をもとに chillSAP という独自コミュニティを発足したり、SAP Inside Track Tokyo の運営をしたり、ブログや SNS によって SAP ビジネスにおけるキャリアを成功された方です。すみっこさんの BIF ブログを、ぜひお楽しみに！

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

I am translating my Japanese text into English using Amazon Q Business. Also, the views and opinions expressed in this blog are my own and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of my employer.

Hello everyone. I am Tetsuya Kawahara from Amazon Web Services Japan. While I don't have much direct business interaction, I am writing a blog after being nominated for the SAP Community's Blog It Forward (BIF) by Mr. Kinoshita (@Shiroh44) from SAP Japan, whom I know through the Japan SAP Users' Group (JSUG) and social media. You can find Mr. Kinoshita's BIF blog here and details about BIF here.

A while ago, I read a very interesting article titled 'How Many People in Japan Speak English?' According to it, only 2% of Japanese people have advanced English proficiency. Additionally, 31-41% of Japanese people cannot speak English at all. This is why I am writing this blog in Japanese first.

About Me

Let me briefly look back on my career centered around SAP business. Like many Japanese people, I'm not good at English, and because I didn't want to (or couldn't) work in English, I joined Fujitsu, a Japanese company, after graduating from university. While it's changing recently, Japan's unique culture includes mass hiring of new graduates where companies recruit students by academic year, and the employment practice based on lifetime employment. At Fujitsu, I belonged to the SAP Competence Center, where I developed and promoted platform solutions for SAP combining our own products with various ISV products. I worked with partners providing OS/DB and virtualization technologies like Microsoft, Oracle, Red Hat, and VMware, as well as ISVs offering backup, operations management, and security products. We were among the first in Japan to verify new products and technologies, develop solutions, and implement them for customers. I've also been active in the Japanese SAP community as an opinion leader in the JSUG Technical Special Interest Group. Through the JSUG International Dispatch Program, I attended SAP TechEd multiple times in the US (Las Vegas), Spain (Barcelona), and India (Bangalore). This photo was taken at the SAP TechEd venue. My experience working broadly with infrastructure and partner business as an SAP Basis forms the foundation of my current career.

In 2015, as cloud computing began emerging in the enterprise sector, I moved to my current position at Amazon Web Services Japan, where I now lead the Partner Solutions Architect team responsible for technology alliances with AWS software partners, including SAP. In addition to promoting traditional large-scale SAP S/4HANA on AWS using Amazon EC2 High Memory instances, I'm also working on new businesses like RISE with SAP on AWS and SAP BTP on AWS. The role of SAP Basis has evolved - AWS Launch Wizard for SAP enables step-by-step automation of SAP environment deployment, and with RISE with SAP, SAP provides it as a managed service. How will our work change going forward? Skills to design, build, and operate cloud-native peripheral systems centered around SAP as a core system are in demand. While some may choose to specialize deeply in SAP, since I'm working for AWS, I try to comprehensively follow both technology and business. For example, to promote SaaS adoption in Japan, I published a translation of 'Building multi-tenant SaaS architectures,' authored by Tod Golding who leads AWS SaaS Factory. I'm also expanding my activities to promote cloud marketplace business through AWS Marketplace.

Outside of work, I love music, especially hard rock and heavy metal (HR/HM). I've been playing electric guitar since junior high school. My current favorite guitar is the Wolfgang Special, Eddie Van Halen's model. This is purely my opinion, but I feel many Japanese IT engineers like HR/HM. Speaking of SAP and Germany, German metal bands like Helloween, Gamma Ray, Scorpions, Blind Guardian, Fair Warning, and Edguy are popular in Japan. I think this is because Japan and Germany have similar sensibilities for melody. The intricate sound creation and lyrical melody lines inherited from classical composers like Bach and Beethoven resonate deeply.

The world changed dramatically with COVID-19 from late 2019, but daily life has been returning since the Japanese government reclassified COVID-19 as a Class 5 disease in May 2023. During the pandemic, all overseas artists' visits to Japan were canceled, making me realize the importance of meeting when possible. Experience cannot be replaced by anything. Recently, I try to attend concerts when I can, like MR.BIG's The BIG Finale! Forever In Our Hearts at Nippon Budokan, concluding their farewell tour. Even for non-farewell tours, we never know when they'll come to Japan next or if they'll come with the same members. My opinion is 'Support your favorites while you can!'

Now, I'd like to pick up and answer some BIF questions.

If you were 20 again, what would you study?

Indeed, it's about English. I often think how much easier my current work would be if I had been born in an English-speaking country, let alone being 20 years old there. In Japan, since we can receive higher education up to university level in Japanese, there aren't many opportunities to learn or use English unless we take initiative. While machine translation quality has improved recently making things somewhat easier, I feel there's a 10-fold difference in productivity when writing or discussing in Japanese versus English. According to the language difficulty ranking published by America's Foreign Service Institute (FSI), Japanese is categorized as Category 4, the most difficult for English speakers. Japanese sentence structure is basically SOV (Subject-Object-Verb) compared to English's SVO (Subject-Verb-Object), making the way we construct sentences different. Additionally, Japanese pronunciation only has five vowel sounds ('a-i-u-e-o'), making it difficult not only to speak but even to understand English. There's never enough time for Japanese people to learn English.

What do you enjoy most in your work and why?

It's about always working on new things. As I mentioned in the career section, I have been involved with various new products and technologies as an SAP Basis professional and solution architect. It's very rewarding work to co-develop offerings that solve customers' business challenges by combining not only our own products but also partner solutions. AWS has over 200 cloud services, and AWS Marketplace has more than 20,000 partner solutions across more than 70 product categories. This partner community holds unlimited potential. One of Amazon's Leadership Principles is 'Learn and Be Curious - Leaders are never done learning and always seek to improve themselves. They are curious about new possibilities and act to explore them.' I want to always maintain this intellectual curiosity.

What do you imagine or predict is the future of your topic?

We're moving from a cloud-first era to an AI-first era. Generative AI has already become indispensable in business - enhancing customer experiences by adding value to services, streamlining routine tasks like report and document creation, and improving development productivity by automating everything from coding to testing, fixes, and operations. In the world of Agentic AI, agents will autonomously execute multiple complex tasks while making decisions. There's discussion about 'SaaS is Dead,' but this doesn't mean SaaS will disappear. Rather, I believe the combination of SaaS and AI will become standard, evolving toward agents calling SaaS solutions that can solve business challenges. We may need to redesign products and customer experiences, considering how to create SaaS that agents will choose and how to provide APIs and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers that agents can call. It would be interesting to predict how SAP's product strategy will develop from this perspective. From on-premises to cloud, from packages to SaaS, and from AI prompts to agents - I look forward to enjoying this turning point in our era.

How was my BIF blog? Next, I nominate Mr. Otsuka (@sumikko), known as 'SAP's Sumikko-san.' He is renowned as a blogger in Japan's SAP community, and has built a successful career in SAP business through his activities - establishing his own community called chillSAP based on the network he cultivated, managing SAP Inside Track Tokyo, and utilizing blogs and social media. Please look forward to Sumikko-san's BIF blog!

entry.title: Re: How to remove '#' (hash) from text line in print outp...
entry.content:
Hi All,

While working in ABAP, especially when preparing data for file downloads or exporting internal tables, you might notice that some lines seem to end with a " # " symbol. But here's the catch  that " # " is not always a real hash symbol. Naturally, you might think it’s a hashtag — but often, it’s not. These characters don’t display visually, but depending on how the file is opened or downloaded, they may appear as strange or misleading symbols.

Check out my post for a detailed explanation of How to Remove Hashtag (#) Using ABAP – It’s Not Always a Hashtag! 

I hope this helps! Let me know your thoughts or if you have any questions.
Thanks,

Akshay Anil   Linkedin Profile
Technical Consultant, SAP ABAP | BTP | Fiori | Ui5

entry.title: CF Deployment failed with DCL files deployment in Cloud Identity Service instance BTP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I was having issues deploying my CAP application along with DCL files for achieving RBAC & ABAC (Authorization checks) to BTP, running into a deploy issue:

So I started from scratch with a simple application: 

- Install a fresh Trial Account

- Create a HANA DB (mapping to CF dev space)

- Create a simple CAP application in BAS, single table, simple service to display table entries

- Add hana (for production), Add mta

- Create MTA file

- Deploy to CF

I referred the below url’s for adding & deploying  DCL files with AMS authoization checks(RBAC & ABAC).

 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/cap-java-implementation-for-authorization-...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/authorization-management-service-in-sap-cl...

 

All vanilla steps, explained in many blogs and CAPIRE. Nothing special, no manipulation of any package.json or any other files. 

However, no matter what I do. I always run into a CF deploy issue  :

I am trying to explore the concept of AMS(Authoriztion Management System) with RBAC& ABAC. I have created the CAPM application in SAP BTP by using BAS(Business Application Studio). Also, I have configured the deployment properties in mta.yaml file . The application is deployed with Cloud Identity Service instance in CF(Cloud Foundry ) environment with out any issues. But, DCL files are not deployed and getting the errors . I tried out in different ways and failed. Added the logs below for your reference:

entry.title: HBA Approval button is disabled for some higher-level managers
entry.content:
Issue: When using Hierarchy Based Approval (HBA) the Approval button is disabled for some higher-level managers.

The cause seems to be the higher-level managers are not eligible for the programs even though their direct reports are eligible.  As a result, forms are not created for these managers and some forms in the hierarchy are missing.

The solution is quite simple.  If you make the managers compensation eligible, they will appear on the forms when you create forms from the top of the hierarchy.  It is important to also ensure these managers are set to be Salary Tab ineligible.  Making them Salary Tab ineligible ensures that budgets are not impacted, and planning cannot be done for those employees.  By doing this, we ensure the Approval button is enabled.

How do you set this eligibility?  You have two options and either one will work just as well.

  1. You can create an eligibility rule and manage the permissions. 
    1. From the template, navigate to Plan Setup; Plan Details; Eligibility
    2. Select a rule and choose Yes for Compensation Eligibility and No for Salary Tab Eligibility

       

  2. If you have a field in Employee Central or a MDF Table which will determine eligibility you can set the eligibility in the Design Worksheet.
    1. From the template, navigate to Plan Setup; Design Worksheet; Design Worksheet
    2. From the drop down that defaults to Form Fields choose Eligibility Fields
    3. Click Add Eligibility Column
    4. Choose Compensation and Salary (Tab) and Click Done
    5. For Each field, map the eligibility to the correct field in EC or on the MDF

Cloud Integration - How to Setup Secure HTTP Inbou... - SAP Community

To configure and deploy Integrations flows in WebUI your user needs the Group Role AuthGroup.IntegrationDeveloper or Single Roles WebToolingWorkspace.Read,

NodeManager.deploycontent.

Thansk you @alex_bundschuh

entry.title: Re: SAP build work zone, Custom Visualization of Tile is...
entry.content:
@dipender002 I experienced same before. As it is generic tile, you should implement press event of GenericTile Control to perform an action in your custom tile:

Consider that it's your view for custom tile:

<mvc:View
    controllerName="peotile.controller.Tile"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    displayBlock="true"
    xmlns:microchart="sap.suite.ui.microchart"
    xmlns="sap.m"
>
    <GenericTile id="idTileMetricHealty"
        header="{i18n>genericTile-title}"
        sizeBehavior="Responsive"
        press="onPress"
        frameType="OneByOne"
    >
        <TileContent id="idTileContent">
            <microchart:HarveyBallMicroChart
                id="idTileHarveyBallMicroChart"
                total="100"
                showFractions="false"
                showTotal="false"
                size="Responsive"
                alignContent="Left"
            >
                <microchart:items>
                    <microchart:HarveyBallMicroChartItem
                        id="idTileHarveyBallMicroChartItem"
                        fraction="100"
                        formattedLabel="true"
                        color="Good"
                    />
                </microchart:items>
            </microchart:HarveyBallMicroChart>
        </TileContent>
    </GenericTile>
</mvc:View>

and its controller:

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageToast",
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
], function (
    MessageToast, Controller
) {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("peotile.controller.Tile", {

        onInit: function () {

        },
        onPress: function () {
            MessageToast.show("Pressed");
        },

entry.title: How to return an object code using the Lookup() in business rule: The use case of Payment Info Bank
entry.content:
If you enjoy my blogs, please let me know by clicking on the Kudo button just above! Your support inspires me to write more…

 

Let’s start!

I'm a big fan of lookup functions (yes, this is going to be a geeky post!)… Whether it is in Excel or in SuccessFactors, they're super handy tools to have in your skillset as they make it easy to find the information you need quickly and efficiently, which is really helpful in any work setting.

 

But…

In SuccessFactors, there is a limitation when trying to use the lookup function to retrieve the external code of a generic object for a field type of that same object. This limitation can be frustrating as it could be very helpful in certain situations or for meeting specific business needs.

 

But… what do you mean in English?!?

Here are two simple examples where I can't achieve my desired outcome using the lookup() function.

In the first scenario, when I try to find a Bank in the payment information using the routing number for lookup, the Select option doesn't show any matching fields:

In the second scenario, when I search for a department where the department's chief position matches the employee supervisor's position, the system only offers the Parent Department as a returnable value instead of just the Department itself, which is what I need:

With these outcomes, I can't help but feel a bit let down by the lookup function in SuccessFactors...

 

Please find a work around!!!

Let’s focus on the Payment Information if you are using Banks in SuccessFactors case. The steps would involves the employee or new hire selecting a bank from the list, which then populates the routing number. While this process sounds great in theory, the reality is that users can feel overwhelmed when they click on the dropdowns, especially when faced with lists containing thousands of values.

Therefore, one of our clients' top requests was to flip this way around. They wanted (or begged!) a process where employees or new hires could enter their routing number first and then watch as the system worked its magic to validate the bank's existence in the bank list and return the match. It sounded like a reasonable ask as if I was the end user, this would make much more sense to me so, I decided to take some time to dig deeper in that Lookup() not being to Select the attribute I needed to solve this fun new challenge.

 

Assumption as of why is the External Code not available to pick in the Lookup()?

While I haven't come across this in written documentation, here is my conclusion based on my investigation of why the Select comes back empty or without the object external code. It all boils down to the field type. When attempting to set a field, the lookup Select presents us with all the fields from the selected object that match the field type we are trying to set. In the case of setting the Payment Information Details.Bank field, the Lookup Select doesn't suggest any field to return because there isn't a field in the Bank object configuration that is a Generic Object type field referencing a Bank.

The last statement sound weird but it is true! If we look at the configuration of Bank the externalCode is a String. Hence, a String can’t be Select to return a Generic Object type field.

 

Let’s validate this assumption

Let’s confirm this theory by adding a new field on the Bank object (Field Type Generic Object of type Bank) and validate the field is available in the lookup Select now. It is! As per screenshot below!

Great, we understand the why! What should we do next?

 

And why not just try this…

Now, we have a custom field called Bank External Code, with the correct field type on our Bank object for our lookup to Select BUT we need to populate that field! Could we just populate our custom field with the value from the Bank externalCode (the String one)? I know it is weird logic but hey… if it works, we will take it!

Let's proceed by adding an onSave rule to the Bank object that saves the Bank Code (standard externalCode field of type String) into the Bank External Code (custom field of type Generic Object) and look at the outcomes.

 

It worked! With the return value now accessible, now let’s wrap things up and build the rule on the Payment Information portlet. The goal (if we remember) is that the employee input his Routing Number and then, the Bank populated automatically through our look rule. Here is the rule I used as an example:

And here are the successful results! While it may be challenging to demonstrate visually, I can assure you that by simply changing the routing number, the bank field was automatically updated! Trust me on this one...

 

 

In conclusion

I guess, although not perfect, I can still still a fan of Lookup() within SuccessFactors… On a serious note, I am genuinely thrilled with this finding!

The ability to retrieve external code values from standard object tables in the Lookup() through this configuration tweak is truly valuable, especially considering the various scenarios where such functionality is essential.

I hope you found this blog insightful and enjoyable, Kudo it if you did!

Stéphanie

entry.title: Key-user extensibility and updating fields
entry.content:
Hi,

I have recently added a field to a Fiori App using Key-user extensibility. However, in practice, this field will only be updated via calles to an API from an external system. I was therefore expecting (as the field is added to the table and various views, and the puiblic RAP API) that I would be able to update this field using the modern RAP model via EML statements using (in my case) I_EquipmentTP.

Indeed, in the past when adding a field to a standard table, you would almost always be able to update it using a BAPI via the extension tables. I was expecting a similar option in the new RAP model, where fields can be added using key-user extensibility and then be updated via the standard Fiori apps and the corresponding standard RAP objects.

However this is apparently not the case.

Is there a vision on this from SAP? Is it just a special case I have? I would expect that fields added to FIori apps via Key-user extensibility can also be updated in those Fiori apps, otherwise, what is the point of adding them?

Any input on this?

Thomas

entry.title: Revenue Sharing Across Business Units from Main Contract in SAP BRIM: CC or CI?
entry.content:
We are working on a SAP BRIM implementation where a customer has a main contract that should distribute monthly usage charges across multiple Lines of Business (LOBs), each with a specific percentage.

The number of LOBs linked to a contract is dynamic.

We are evaluating two approaches:

1. Separate contracts per LOB via SOM
Each LOB is represented by its own provider contract in SOM, mirroring the main contract. A fulfillment step creates allowances in CC for each LOB with its associated percentage. SAP CC then calculates and generates individual Billable Items (BITs) for each LOB according to their allowance and rate.

2. Single BIT in CC and split via CI
SAP CC generates one BIT with the full monthly amount. In SAP Convergent Invoicing (CI), a custom table defines the LOB-to-percentage mapping. CI uses this to create Dependent Items (DITTYPE), distributing the total amount across LOBs according to their configured share.

We’d appreciate feedback from anyone who has handled a similar scenario:

  • Would you recommend implementing the logic in CC or in CI?

  • Is there a best practice or alternative approach we’re overlooking?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Get ready for Joule: Transition to IAS/IPS for enhanced security and integration
entry.content:
What is IAS / IPS?  

SAP Cloud Identity Services, within SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), is a prerequisite for Joule and other SAP cloud solutions. 

It comprises of two components: Identity Authentication Services (IAS), responsible for authentication / logging user in. Identity Provisioning Services (IPS), is a companion tool to IAS, used to sync users from the source (e.g. SAP SuccessFactors HCM) to the target (e.g. IAS), for them to be authenticated / logged in. 
These services offer advanced identity management features, multifactor and risk-based authentication, as well as a robust and secure user login experience, using open industry standard protocols (e.g. SCIM, SAML, OAuth).

Why migrate to IAS / IPS? 

Basic Authentication and third-party corporate identity provider (IdP) direct integration with SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite will reach end of maintenance and support on June 2, 2025, and be deleted on November 1, 2026. See: Community Blog. 

After June 2, 2025, users of those customers still using the old SSO certificate and not integrated with SAP Cloud Identity Services - IAS, will no longer be able to access SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite causing downtime for the system. 

Once the depreciation date is reached, SAP will no longer support the use of Basic Authentication (username and password); and third-party corporate identity providers (IdP) direction integration with SuccessFactors without SAP Cloud Identity Services as proxy. 

Basic authentication provides customers with less security than the common methods of authentication used by many cloud applications, since there are no additional mechanisms to verify the user, and passwords are often reused across multiple accounts and applications, increasing the risk of hacking. 

Third-party corporate identity provider (IdP) allows direct integration with SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite in exchange for Identity Authentication as the main or proxy IdP will unify the user login experience across all SAP applications. It also enables the harmonization of UI and API authentication for SAP applications to the same platform to facilitate better authorization management for users.  Moreover, this will reduce maintenance efforts between SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite and corporate identity providers since there are pre-built integrations and provisioning of Identity Authentication to SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite and other SAP applications. 
 

How to migrate to IAS / IPS? 

SAP SuccessFactors HCM 

Customers can follow the step-by-step E-learning to learn how to integrate SAP SuccessFactors HCM with SAP Cloud Identity Services.  

If customers need additional support, they can register to attend regular Office Hours featuring topic-specific experts.  

Attendees will need to Sign-Up to the SAP Learning Hub with a valid s-user to register. 

Note that this migration can be done via the Upgrade Center, without SAP Consultants and should involve the customer’s IT department. This is not generally an activity that HR Admins can do on their own. 

SAP SuccessFactors HCM tenants provisioned after Dec 9, 2022, are already pre-provisioned with IAS/IPS. 

SAP Ariba and Fieldglass 

Customers can watch these Session 1 and Session 2  to be introduced to IAS/IPS concepts from an SAP Ariba and Fieldglass perspective. Note that these recordings are hosted in the ON24 platform and registration is required. 

If customers need additional support, they can register to regular Office Hours featuring topic-specific experts. Attendees will need to Sign-Up to the SAP Learning Hub with a valid s-user to register.

entry.title: Re: remove material master mrp1 tab
entry.content:
SAP does not allow the deletion of individual views once they are created in standard transactions like MM02.

But you can handle this in a way:

Set MRP Type to "ND" (No Planning)

This is the standard and safest way to deactivate MRP functionality:

  • Go to MM02 (Change Material).

  • Navigate to the MRP1 tab.

  • Set MRP Type = ND (No planning).

  • Remove any lot sizing, procurement type, etc.

    This effectively deactivates MRP for the material without actually removing the tab.

    It’s commonly used for non-MRP-managed items.

There should be no Iowa taxable income and was not until the 12/10/21 paycheck. The only thing I found was WT8115 (OT Premium) totaled $0.31 -- the same amount as the Iowa taxable income.

The 12/10/21 paycheck did have many IT2010 records entered for past pay periods, which we believe contributed to the issue.

Does anyone know what would cause the IA taxable income to suddenly appear when nothing was changed with this coworker's Resident and Work state?

Thanks!

entry.title: MaCo Onboarding Agent: Exploring its Multi-Agent AI Architecture
entry.content:
Overview

As AI-driven transformation reshapes enterprise systems, intelligent onboarding becomes essential for improving customer experience and accelerating product adoption. In MaCo (SAP Market Communication for Utilities), we are driving an innovation initiative by developing the MaCo Onboarding Agent—a multi-agent AI solution.

The MaCo Onboarding Agent is designed to engage with customers before they subscribe to MaCo saas. On request, the agent is intended to collect essential information such as Global Account, subaccount, and subdomain, and generate an onboarding incident to initiate and manage the onboarding process. This removes manual overhead, especially for customers with multiple subscriptions.

Using a vectorized knowledge base from MaCo documentation, downstream service documentation, and FAQs, the assistant offers real-time, context-specific support. For example, based on the customer's monthly message volume, the assistant can recommend the appropriate subscription plan, for testing or production use, by referencing the relevant information maintained in the FAQs. This ensures customers receive timely and contextually accurate guidance, improving their overall experience.

Architecture: Engineered for Scalability, Modularity, and Intelligence

The MaCo Onboarding Agent is built on a supervisor-level multi-agent architecture, designed with a modular and extensible technology stack:

  • Python with LangChain and LangGraph: Serves as the orchestration layer for agent-based interactions. LangChain provides structured tooling for chaining LLM prompts and external calls, while LangGraph introduces persistent, event-driven workflows with stateful memory management and multi-agent routing logic, exposed as a RESTful API.
  • SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming) using Node.js
  • SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform)
  • SAP HANA for Semantic Search and Context Retrieval 
    MaCo documentation, downstream service documentations and references, and FAQs are converted into vector representations stored in SAP HANA's vector database. This allows the assistant to perform semantic searches and retrieve information based on meaning rather than exact keywords. At runtime, relevant content is dynamically retrieved from the vector store to build context for responses.
  • Multi-Model Support (e.g., GPT-4.0, Gemini): Enables runtime selection of multiple large language models based on use case requirements such as accuracy vs. latency trade-offs, tokens constraints, and response time requirements. The classification logic is also handled by LLMs, based on the prompt and input context.

User Interface

 

 

What’s in Progress

The MaCo Onboarding Agent extends its capabilities beyond a standard chatbot assistant. Integration with JIRA and ServiceNow APIs is also underway, enabling a dedicated agent to create and manage incidents.

Business Benefits

Efficient Onboarding Process

  • Accelerates the onboarding process by providing context-specific support and resolving queries in real-time
  • Particularly valuable for enterprise customers with high-volume onboarding requirements by automating manual tasks.

24/7 Real-Time Support

  • Leverages a vectorized knowledge base for real-time, context-aware assistance.
  • Once subscribed, customers go through multiple setup steps. The assistant provides on-demand access to knowledge from MaCo documentation, downstream service references, and FAQs—enabling users to resolve known issues or questions independently. This reduces reliance on support teams and minimizes delays caused by time zone differences or limited team availability.

Continuous Customer Engagement

  • Keeps customers informed on ongoing issues with downstream services or MaCo issues by gathering information from FAQs.
  • Ensures ongoing transparency and trust during the onboarding lifecycle.

Conclusion

The MaCo Onboarding Agent demonstrates how intelligent automation and a multi-agent solution can enhance the efficiency and consistency of early customer interactions. By providing access to relevant information and supporting decision-making with contextual guidance, the solution enables a more scalable and responsive customer experience.

Ongoing enhancements would explore extended capabilities, including deeper integrations, improved coordination between agents, and alignment with evolving customer and product needs.

entry.title: SAP CALM alerts for SAP Data Intelligence (DI) and SAP cloud HANA
entry.content:
We have enabled SAP CALM alerts for SAP DI and SAAP Hana cloud database on BTP sub account. We could like to understand how the alerts are triggered and do we have any option to create custom events or metrics in CALM health monitoring.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP CALM alerts for SAP Data Intelligence (DI) and SA...
entry.content:
In checking Expert Portal: https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/setup-managed-services/setup-di.html -> SAP Data Intelligence is only supported with Operations use case Integration & Exception Monitoring, so you will not have access to this service in Health Monitoring.

There are several resources with information of your queries about event configuration for Health Monitoring and Integration and Exception Monitoring, these are two different things although similarly configured, that I would refer you to:

  • KBA 3482250 - How the event setting works for the Integration Monitoring - SAP Cloud ALM -> has link to configuration guide:  https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/im-configuring-events
  • KBA 3436616 - How to configure SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring metric thresholds, alerts, and e-mail notifications?
  • KBA 3543572 - Relation between Metrics and Events in Health Monitoring application in Cloud ALM -> which has link to KBA : 3398745 - Can custom Events be defined for missing metrics in the various SAP Cloud ALM Use Cases? -> Health Monitoring does not allow custom metrics to be defined in IEP.
  • KBA 3445128 - SAP Cloud ALM Integration & Exception Monitoring Help

SAP Community offers blog:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/cloud-alm-health-monitoring-for-event-configuration/ba-p/13575560
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/monitoring-integration-issues-with-the-new-issues-detected-event-in-sap/ba-p/13964435 (link at bottom to setting up events in SAP Help Portal)
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/configuring-sap-cloud-alm-for-integration-and-exception-third-party/ba-p/13709340

I believe you will find the information needed here.

Best regards,
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Re: required data does not exist in this plant IAI1 for t...
entry.content:
To solve this question, the following solutions may work

1. Extend Material to Plant IAI1

  • Transaction: MM01 (Create Material) or MM02 (Change Material)

  • Enter the material number, industry sector, and material type

  • Select the required views (Basic Data, MRP, Sales, etc.)

  • Enter plant IAI1 and other relevant organizational levels

  • Maintain all mandatory fields and save

2. Check Existing Material Data

  • Transaction: MM03 (Display Material)

  • Verify if the material is extended to plant IAI1

  • If not, use MM01 to extend it.

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP FIORI APP upgrade impact analysis
entry.content:
Hi,

 

We have upgraded SAP S/4HANA (Sandbox) from 1909 to 2023 FPS2. As we went through the post checks, we have found the following issue.

Made a spot check of F0928A which according to the analysis should not be available. This turned out not to be true, also the Readiness check report did not contain information about the app being unavailable.

  • Fiori apps extracted from our roles and analyzed in the Fiori upgrade impact analysis tool as provided by SAP
  • A list of 41 unavailable APPs identified
  • Random APP amongst the 41 checked in our system and is still seemingly working just fine.

Questions:

  • Are the APPs in the list still available but “not recommended”?
  • Is the list not accurate, “false positives”?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI APP upgrade impact analysis
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

I hope you're doing well.

 

Please note that the upgrade impact analysis tool is provided to make the upgrade process easier.

App F0928A is deprecated as of SAP S/4HANA 2023 and should not be used.

This is also explained in KBA 3574080 - SAP Fiori Upgrade Impact Analysis Report Showing Deprecated/Unavailable Features as Available.

Please keep in mind that all the information about apps in the App Library is provided by the App Teams, not the Fiori Launchpad Team.

Thus, any further questions you need to ask for specific apps need to be addressed to the respective teams, which for F0928A is CO-FIO-BPC-IS.

The tool should not return false positives, but it is entirely dependent on the information published in the Fiori Apps Library by the App Teams.

 

Best regards,
SAP Technical Support / Fiori Launchpad Team

entry.title: Re: Why AW01N show negative Net Book Value
entry.content:
Hi @RTN

Planned depreciation is automatically calculated based on Depreciation key and useful life. If you want to remove planned depreciation, you can just simply change depreciation key to zero depreciation. But make sure your last asset fiscal year is closed as any changes in asset master affect all asset open years.

Check check and confirm the thread as lot of new questions are going on in a single thread. Please respect the community guidelines. Thank you

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: 2H 2024 Release Highlights for the Compensation Module
entry.content:
Hi @35969 

Carbon copy features remains untouched by this enhancement and still only triggers a copy of the worksheet in someone's Completed inbox folder when the worksheet gets routed to completed. Also note that it's all related to the planner used in the workflow so if you use 2nd hierarchy for planning you won't be able to send a copy of the worksheet to hierarchical managers of the employees in the worksheets. Only to someone connected to the 2nd Manager.

Maybe plan for read-only exec review access for regular managers to trigger upon completion.

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: 1° Meetup SAP 2025 no Rio de Janeiro!
entry.content:
Olá a todos,

Estamos muito animados para anunciar o nosso primeiro "Encontro da Comunidade SAP" no Rio de Janeiro no dia 26 de junho! Este evento é uma oportunidade fantástica para todos os profissionais SAP do Rio de Janeiro se reunirem, compartilharem as últimas atualizações e discutirem o crescimento da comunidade SAP na nossa cidade.

Esse encontro será mais uma oportunidade para que você conheça e faça networking com outros entusiastas do SAP que estão fazendo contribuições significativas para os clientes e parceiros do Rio de Janeiro!

É uma chance de se conectar, trocar ideias e construir uma comunidade local SAP mais forte.

Evento:
Data: 26 de maio de 2025
Local: Cafofo Pub, Botafogo, Rio de Janeiro

entry.title: MI07 Problema na atribuição de impostos (J1BTAX) em NF gerada por inventário
entry.content:
Olá, pessoal!
Estou com uma situação no SAP ECC relacionada à nota fiscal gerada automaticamente por inventário, onde apenas o primeiro item da nota tem a redução de base de ICMS corretamente atribuída. Os demais itens assumem grupos fiscais genéricos (10/99), ignorando o cadastro correto da J1BTAX.

Cenário:
A nota é gerada automaticamente após movimentação de inventário (ex: MB1A / 701).

A J1BTAX está corretamente configurada para todos os materiais.

A função J_1B_IM_TX_CALCULATE_TAX_NEW é chamada para o cálculo fiscal, e percebi que o problema acontece no PERFORM get_tax_parameters, onde para os itens a partir do segundo, o sistema já não localiza os grupos corretos.

A estrutura XKOMV[] já chega preenchida na função, mas aparentemente os KSCHL não refletem as condições como acontece na MIRO.

Testes já realizados:
Executei o mesmo cenário via MIRO: os impostos são aplicados corretamente a todos os itens.

Revisei a J_1BCONDMAPV, e os KSCHL estão mapeados corretamente.

Comparei a J1BTAX e o grupo de impostos por material — está tudo certo.

Debuguei a função e percebi que após o 1º item, a lógica de atribuição de grupo fiscal não consegue recuperar os dados da J1BTAX.

Na MIRO, o grupo fiscal é atribuído corretamente a todos os itens — no inventário, apenas ao primeiro.

Pergunta:
Existe alguma particularidade na chamada da função de cálculo de imposto quando a nota é gerada por movimento de mercadoria (inventário), que afete a leitura correta da J1BTAX ou do mapeamento fiscal?

Ou ainda:

Alguma recomendação de nota SAP, configuração complementar ou workaround para garantir que todos os itens da nota de inventário tenham os impostos atribuídos corretamente?

entry.title: External checkbox grayed out when trying to change a number range from internal to external
entry.content:
What would be the best way to merge three EAN number ranges into one, but one of them is internal and already has been used (has a number range status greater than 0).
The external checkbox is grayed out when trying to change the number range internal to external
The system throws error message NR587 "Entered number level is less than the previous level. Interval" if I try to change it to 0.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: External checkbox grayed out when trying to change a...
entry.content:
External checkbox is grayed out because you cannot change an internal number range to external if it has a number range status greater than 0.

Set the number range status to 0, then mark the number range as external and save. 

If you are going to use the number range interval to external, just ignore the warning message NR587. 
Changing number range status to a lower number than it already is may lead to duplicate internal numbering materials, but if the range is not used as an internal number, there's no issue.

To solve any overlap issues and understand better how number range works, please take a look on the following blog post:

  • How does number ranges works in Product Master SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? | SAP Community

Kind Regards, 
Ícaro

entry.title: Re: Internal server error in additional data collection step
entry.content:
Hello!

I had the same problem with my admission.

Reproducing the Scenario:

ONBOARDING 2.0 > Collection of additional data
Step performed by the Onboardee, at which point he fills in the information that will be mapped into the customized MDF objects. When accessing the step, Onboardee encounters the error "Internal Server Error"

Cause:
Some of the custom objects did not have permissions for the ExternalUser

Resolution:
Access the role permission groups of your external users in > Manage permission roles > Access your external user's role permission group Onboardee (in my case: OnboardingExternalUser and also ONB - External Collaborator) > Edit > Next > Miscellaneous permissions > Grant viewing and editing permissions to all custom objects that you need the external user to access > Next > Save

This answer was written in Portuguese and translated to English via Google Translate, so the name of the screens you need to access may be different in your language.

Anyway, I hope I helped.

entry.title: Re: How can you create a Story Report that shows who has...
entry.content:
We had similar issues when trying to use the advanced filter to drill down to the current plan year, I can't tell from your screenshot if you are filtering on this field or not. Once we removed that advanced filter and placed a simple filter on the Primary Objective table (named Primary Goal at my org)  for the plan year, we then saw the employees without any goals. 

One other thing we found in testing was that when starting with the Objective/Goal table we got similar results, only showing those who had goals created. We started with Personal Info, but I would think using the User Info as a starting point would work the same way.

entry.title: How to edit Purchase Order Item Messages ?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Help Community,

I have a question regarding Purchase Order Item Messages and I need your help.

My requirement is to change the type of one item message in the purchase order item messages.

For example here in the picture below the type of the message is Warning message and it is standard message that is going to be shown if the material status is "F2".

The requirement is:

  • when the the delivery plant(EKKO-RESWK) is initial, the message should be displayed as Error message and not as a warning message
  • when the the delivery plant(EKKO-RESWK) is not initial, the message should be not displayed anymore, e.g, the message should be removed from the item messages

So my question is: Can I achieve this requirement via customization(if yes can you please tell me how) or is there any BADI or customer function or user exit in order to edit the purchase order item messages ?

I would be grateful  for any suggestion you may have.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Cash Application
entry.content:

if there is a PDF you can upload it via the Cash Application, you find more information here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CASH_APPLICATION/102154267af9489a88ff32e6416defc3/cb61b966e0a24d8bab288b994f339801.html?q=advice

But what might be also of interest in the future could be the new SAP Document AI: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RRIu5AOwe_I

 

But it's still being enhanced with functionalities. But you should reach out to your account manager and ask about a roadmap with a possible integration intoremittance advices.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha
